summary: Excellent performance with standout jams and a clear matrix recording despite minor tape glitches
review: Kochman's detailed write‑up praises the band’s tight playing, especially Jerry’s soaring leads, the monster tiger section in Playing In The Band and the soulful Wharf Rat/Get Up and Fly Away sequence. The soundboard‑plus‑audience matrix source described by Ines Mullen is generally clear and immersive, only marred by a few audible tape transitions and early‑show sound quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Two in‑depth reviews both award five stars; the musical performance is described as consistently strong with many highlight moments, while the recording is rated good (matrix source, clear overall) – together they merit an above‑average to exceptional rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard base patched with audience fills; generally clear and immersive, but early sound issues and a few tape‑transition clicks are noted.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Wharf Rat / Get Up and Fly Away
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds energy with tight, concise numbers; the second set launches into a sprawling, exploratory jam (notably the tiger section) and flows seamlessly back into Playing In The Band, ending with a soulful Wharf Rat/GET Up and Fly Away sequence before a strong encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is praised throughout – solid effort on The Promised Land, noodle‑rich tapestries in PITB, front‑line leads on Eyes Of The World.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for his solo interplay with Jerry during the Eyes Of The World jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted as a bit over‑amped toward the end of Greatest Story Ever Told, but generally solid rhythm/vocal work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard is described as “too low” on Ship Of Fools, indicating a mix/level issue for that song.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Mickey) gets kudos for putting his all into Playing In The Band.
